ip_outcome_note: Depicted figure confirmed as Garruk Wildspeaker, the warrior-druid planeswalker. Identity is canonical by Wizards-internal naming convention: the card title 'Garruk's Uprising' explicitly names the character, the M21 set features him as one of five planeswalker-deck faces (Basri, Teferi, Liliana, Chandra, Garruk), and the depicted figure (burly bearded man in green-and-brown leathers, axe, accompanied by a beast in a forest) matches MTG Wiki's canonical description of Garruk (human druid, warrior-druid abilities, special affinity with animals and beasts). The M21 print specifically depicts him in his cleansed/'Unleashed' phase — Wizards' product page notes Garruk 'had just lost his curse in Throne of Eldraine and was back to mono-green' for M21. This is a Wizards-internal named character, not an external-IP crossover, so no `## IP` callout block is required. Frontmatter `ip_verified` flipped to true; `ip_confidence` was already `high` and is retained.

trivia_bullets.text: Garruk's Uprising prints in Core Set 2021 (set code M21, released 2020-07-03). M21 has no story spotlights and no overarching narrative — its theme is 'Dogs vs. Cats' and Garruk was included because 'he had just lost his curse in Throne of Eldraine and was back to mono-green. It felt right to have Garruk back in the core set' per Wizards' M21 product overview. [Scryfall: m21/186] [Wizards: 'Core Set 2021 Product Overview', 2020-06-05] [Wizards: Mark Rosewater, 'Need I Say Core', 2020-06-08]
sources: Wizards: Mark Rosewater Need I Say Core 2020-06-08

trivia_bullets.text: The 'Uprising' framing references Garruk's character arc: cursed by Liliana Vess's Chain Veil in an Onakke temple on Shandalar (warping his summoning into demonic black-mana hunting), he was eventually cleansed by the Cauldron of Eternity in the Throne of Eldraine storyline, returning him to mono-green and his beasts in M21. His full canonical arc reads Wildspeaker (Lorwyn-era) -> Cursed/Veil-Cursed (post-Liliana) -> Apex Predator (M15) -> Unleashed (M21, post-cleansing). [MTG Wiki: Garruk] [MTG Wiki: Chain Veil]

Illustrated by Wisnu Tan; uncommon enchantment at {2}{G}. Previewed 2020-06-09 by Santiago Bigatti. The set has two collector-number prints of this card — #186 (standard frame) and #308 (showcase/alt frame) — both same art and rules text per Scryfall and Gatherer. [Scryfall: m21/186] [Gatherer: multiverseid=485509]

EDHREC ranks Garruk's Uprising at #92 overall and lists it as the top-ranked green enchantment with roughly 17% inclusion across approximately 698K of 4.06M tracked decks. In Beast-tribal decks specifically, inclusion climbs to ~79% with 62% synergy. The 'power 4 or greater' threshold is trivial to meet in green, and the trample rider adds evasion to creatures that don't already have it — EDHREC's M21 green set review compared it to Sylvan Library as a snowballing value enchantment. [Scryfall: edhrec_rank=92] [EDHREC: garruks-uprising] [EDHREC: 'Core 2021 Set Review - Green']

Reprint history confirms staple status: New Capenna Commander (NCC #292), Forgotten Realms Commander, Wilds of Eldraine 'Enchanting Tales' bonus sheet, Lorwyn Eclipsed Commander (ECC #109), and Foundations (FDN #220). Reprint frequency in Commander-format products is Wizards' usual signal that a card is a Commander format pillar. [Scryfall: prints for oracleid 3127ae9b-a7a7-43ec-89d7-688f8445b33d]

The M21 #186 print has no flavor text — Scryfall returns no flavor_text field for this card. Card is mechanically focused; the narrative work is done by the title 'Garruk's Uprising' and the art itself rather than a quoted line. [Scryfall: m21/186]
